<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8"/>
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"/>
<title>Dual-Encoder EO-SAR Change Detection</title>
<style>
  :root {
    --bg: #0d1117; --surface: #161b22; --border: #30363d;
    --text: #e6edf3; --muted: #8b949e; --accent: #58a6ff;
    --green: #3fb950; --yellow: #d29922; --red: #f85149;
    --tag-bg: #1f6feb22; --tag-border: #1f6feb;
  }
  * { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
  body { background: var(--bg); color: var(--text); font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; line-height: 1.7; }
  .wrap { max-width: 960px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 48px 24px; }

  /* Hero */
  .hero { text-align: center; padding: 48px 0 40px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); margin-bottom: 48px; }
  .hero h1 { font-size: 2.4rem; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 8px; }
  .hero .sub { color: var(--muted); font-size: 1rem; margin-bottom: 24px; }
  .badges { display: flex; gap: 8px; justify-content: center; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 32px; }
  .badge { background: var(--surface); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 20px; padding: 4px 14px; font-size: 0.78rem; color: var(--muted); }
  .badge.blue { border-color: #1f6feb; color: var(--accent); }
  .badge.red  { border-color: #da3633; color: #f85149; }
  .badge.green { border-color: #238636; color: var(--green); }

  /* Metric hero table */
  .metric-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr); gap: 12px; margin: 0 auto 16px; max-width: 820px; }
  .metric-card { background: var(--surface); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 10px; padding: 16px 10px; text-align: center; }
  .metric-card.highlight { border-color: #238636; background: #0f2d1a; }
  .metric-card .label { font-size: 0.72rem; color: var(--muted); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em; margin-bottom: 6px; }
  .metric-card .before { font-size: 0.9rem; color: var(--muted); text-decoration: line-through; }
  .metric-card .after { font-size: 1.4rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--green); }
  .metric-card .delta { font-size: 0.72rem; color: var(--green); margin-top: 4px; }
  .note { color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.82rem; text-align: center; }

  /* Sections */
  h2 { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; margin: 48px 0 16px; padding-bottom: 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); }
  h3 { font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 600; margin: 28px 0 12px; color: var(--accent); }
  p  { margin-bottom: 14px; color: #c9d1d9; }
  strong { color: var(--text); }

  /* Infographic images */
  .infographic { display: block; margin: 24px auto; border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid var(--border); max-width: 100%; }
  .img-caption { text-align: center; color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.82rem; margin-top: -12px; margin-bottom: 24px; }

  /* Tables */
  table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 16px 0 24px; font-size: 0.9rem; }
  th { background: var(--surface); color: var(--muted); font-weight: 600; text-align: left; padding: 10px 14px; border: 1px solid var(--border); font-size: 0.8rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .04em; }
  td { padding: 10px 14px; border: 1px solid var(--border); color: #c9d1d9; }
  tr:hover td { background: #161b22; }
  td strong { color: var(--green); }

  /* Code */
  pre { background: var(--surface); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; padding: 20px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 0.85rem; color: #c9d1d9; margin: 16px 0 24px; line-height: 1.5; }
  code { font-family: 'SFMono-Regular', Consolas, monospace; }
  p code, td code, li code { background: #1f2937; border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 6px; font-size: 0.85em; color: #e2a97e; }

  /* Callout */
  .callout { border-left: 3px solid var(--accent); background: var(--tag-bg); border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0; padding: 14px 18px; margin: 16px 0 24px; color: #c9d1d9; font-size: 0.92rem; }
  .callout.warn { border-color: var(--yellow); background: #d2992210; }
  .callout.success { border-color: var(--green); background: #3fb95010; }

  /* Results grid */
  .results-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 20px; margin: 24px 0; }
  .results-card { background: var(--surface); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 10px; padding: 20px; }
  .results-card h4 { font-size: 0.9rem; color: var(--muted); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em; margin-bottom: 14px; }

  /* Exp cards */
  .exp-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 16px; margin: 20px 0; }
  .exp-card { background: var(--surface); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; }
  .exp-card .num { font-size: 0.72rem; color: var(--muted); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing:.05em; margin-bottom:6px; }
  .exp-card .title { font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 8px; font-size: 0.95rem; }
  .exp-card .iou { font-size: 0.82rem; color: var(--muted); margin-bottom: 8px; }
  .exp-card .finding { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #c9d1d9; border-top: 1px solid var(--border); padding-top: 8px; margin-top: 8px; }
  .exp-card.key { border-color: #d29922; background: #1a160a; }

  /* Lists */
  ul, ol { padding-left: 24px; color: #c9d1d9; margin-bottom: 16px; }
  li { margin-bottom: 6px; }

  /* References */
  .ref-list { list-style: none; padding: 0; counter-reset: refs; }
  .ref-list li { counter-increment: refs; padding: 10px 0 10px 36px; position: relative; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); font-size: 0.88rem; color: #c9d1d9; }
  .ref-list li::before { content: '[' counter(refs) ']'; position: absolute; left: 0; color: var(--accent); font-weight: 600; }

  /* Footer */
  footer { text-align: center; color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.8rem; margin-top: 64px; padding-top: 24px; border-top: 1px solid var(--border); }
</style>
</head>
<body>
<div class="wrap">

<!-- ── HERO ─────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<div class="hero">
  <h1>🛰️ Dual-Encoder EO-SAR Change Detection</h1>
  <p class="sub">Binary pixel-level disaster damage detection on heterogeneous satellite image pairs</p>
  <div class="badges">
    <span class="badge blue">Python 3.10+</span>
    <span class="badge red">PyTorch 2.x</span>
    <span class="badge green">MIT License</span>
    <span class="badge">NVIDIA T4 16GB</span>
  </div>

  <div class="metric-grid">
    <div class="metric-card highlight">
      <div class="label">Test IoU</div>
      <div class="before">0.0139</div>
      <div class="after">0.3230</div>
      <div class="delta">+2,224% · 23×</div>
    </div>
    <div class="metric-card highlight">
      <div class="label">Test Precision</div>
      <div class="before">0.0145</div>
      <div class="after">0.4197</div>
      <div class="delta">+2,794%</div>
    </div>
    <div class="metric-card highlight">
      <div class="label">Test Recall</div>
      <div class="before">0.2520</div>
      <div class="after">0.5837</div>
      <div class="delta">+132%</div>
    </div>
    <div class="metric-card highlight">
      <div class="label">Test F1</div>
      <div class="before">0.0274</div>
      <div class="after">0.4883</div>
      <div class="delta">+1,682%</div>
    </div>
    <div class="metric-card highlight">
      <div class="label">Val IoU</div>
      <div class="before">~0.49</div>
      <div class="after">0.6050</div>
      <div class="delta">+23%</div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <p class="note">Evaluated on scene_09 — fully unseen geography, never seen during training or hyperparameter selection.</p>
</div>

<!-- ── OVERVIEW ──────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Overview</h2>
<p>This repository implements a <strong>dual-encoder deep learning architecture</strong> for binary change detection on heterogeneous <strong>Electro-Optical (EO) pre-event</strong> and <strong>Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) post-event</strong> satellite image pairs. The task: given a colour photograph taken before a disaster and a radar image taken after, produce a pixel-level binary mask identifying exactly where physical destruction occurred.</p>
<p>The core insight is architectural: <strong>EO and SAR measure fundamentally different physical phenomena and must be processed by separate, specialised encoders before fusion.</strong> A single shared encoder — the standard approach — is forced into an irreconcilable compromise between optical feature extraction and radar statistics, causing catastrophic generalisation failure on unseen geographies. The dual-encoder design resolves this, achieving a <strong>23× improvement in Test IoU</strong> on a completely unseen test scene.</p>

<!-- ── PROBLEM ────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>The Problem: Heterogeneous Change Detection</h2>
<p>Standard change detection compares two images from the <strong>same sensor type</strong> at different times. This dataset does not allow that — the pre-event image is EO (optical colour photograph) and the post-event image is SAR (microwave backscatter). Even in completely undamaged regions, EO and SAR images look radically different — not because anything changed, but because the sensors measure different physical quantities. <strong>Naively subtracting EO from SAR is equivalent to subtracting metres from kilograms.</strong></p>

<table>
  <tr><th>Damage Type</th><th>SAR Response</th><th>EO Response</th><th>Engineered Signal</th></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Collapsed building (rubble)</strong></td><td>Dramatically high backscatter — chaotic scattering</td><td>Dull grey, similar to pre-disaster</td><td>Large positive cross-diff</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Flood inundation</strong></td><td>Near-zero — still water reflects radar away</td><td>Medium brightness (pre-disaster soil/vegetation)</td><td>Large negative cross-diff</td></tr>
  <tr><td><strong>Intact structures</strong></td><td>Consistent with structure geometry</td><td>Consistent with optical appearance</td><td>Cross-diff near zero</td></tr>
</table>

<!-- ── ARCHITECTURE ──────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Architecture</h2>
<img class="infographic" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Dual-Encoder Architecture for Change Detection" width="580"/>
<p class="img-caption">Figure 1 — Dual-Encoder Architecture for Change Detection</p>

<h3>Dual-Encoder ResNet34-UNet (V9)</h3>
<pre><code>Input
├── EO Stream: [R, G, B]  →  ResNet34 Encoder (ImageNet pretrained, unmodified)
│                              ↓ s0(64) → s1(64) → s2(128) → s3(256) → bottleneck(512)
└── SAR Stream: [SAR_norm, EO_lum, cross_diff, cross_abs, NDVI, SAR-NDVI]
                            →  ResNet34 Encoder (ImageNet pretrained, conv1 adapted)
                               ↓ s0(64) → s1(64) → s2(128) → s3(256) → bottleneck(512)
Fusion
└── Bottleneck: concat EO(512) + SAR(512) = 1024ch → CBAM Attention
    └── Decoder (FusionUpBlock)
        ├── d3: up(1024→512) + skip[EO_s3+SAR_s3] → 256ch → CBAM
        ├── d2: up(256→128)  + skip[EO_s2+SAR_s2] → 128ch → CBAM
        ├── d1: up(128→64)   + skip[EO_s1+SAR_s1] →  64ch
        └── d0: up(64→32)    + skip[EO_s0+SAR_s0] →  32ch
            └── ConvTranspose2d → Conv1×1 → logits (B, 1, H, W)</code></pre>

<p><strong>Symmetric encoders (both ResNet34):</strong> Matched representational capacity ensures neither modality dominates the fused representation. Earlier asymmetric ResNet34+ResNet18 configurations produced an EO-biased representation that underweighted SAR damage evidence.</p>
<p><strong>CBAM placement (bottleneck + d3/d2 only):</strong> Bottleneck CBAM performs cross-modal re-weighting. Decoder CBAM at d3/d2 refines spatial alignment at high-level semantic scales. Disabled at d1/d0 where global alignment is complete. Experiment 3 confirmed decoder CBAM is a <strong>critical generalisation mechanism</strong> — its removal dropped Test IoU from ~0.27 to ~0.18 while Val IoU stayed stable.</p>

<!-- ── FEATURE ENGINEERING ────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Physics-Grounded Feature Engineering</h2>
<img class="infographic" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Distribution of Input Channels" width="640"/>
<p class="img-caption">Figure 2 — Distribution of Input Channels in V9 Dual-Encoder Architecture</p>

<table>
  <tr><th>#</th><th>Channel</th><th>Formula</th><th>Physical Meaning</th></tr>
  <tr><td>1–3</td><td>EO R, G, B</td><td><code>pre / 255.0</code></td><td>Optical colour</td></tr>
  <tr><td>4</td><td>SAR normalised</td><td><code>(post − 1) / 230.0</code></td><td>Radar backscatter intensity</td></tr>
  <tr><td>5</td><td>EO luminance</td><td><code>0.299R + 0.587G + 0.114B</code></td><td>Pre-event perceptual brightness</td></tr>
  <tr><td>6</td><td>Cross-modal diff</td><td><code>SAR_norm − EO_lum</code></td><td>+ve = rubble, −ve = flood, ~0 = intact</td></tr>
  <tr><td>7</td><td>Cross abs</td><td><code>|cross_diff|</code></td><td>Change magnitude (direction-agnostic)</td></tr>
  <tr><td>8</td><td>NDVI</td><td><code>(G − R) / (G + R + ε)</code></td><td>Pre-event vegetation health</td></tr>
  <tr><td>9</td><td>SAR-NDVI</td><td><code>SAR × (1 − clip(NDVI, 0, 1))</code></td><td>Destruction signature: high SAR + low NDVI</td></tr>
</table>
<p>The EO encoder receives channels 1–3. The SAR encoder receives channels 4–9.</p>

<!-- ── LOSS ───────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Loss Function</h2>
<pre><code>Loss = 0.3 × FocalBCE + 0.7 × FocalTversky(α=0.3, β=0.7, γ=0.75)</code></pre>
<p><strong>Focal Tversky</strong> (dominant, 70%): Up-weights false negatives — missing a destroyed building in disaster response is operationally more costly than a false alarm. β=0.7 &gt; α=0.3 encodes this asymmetry directly.</p>
<p><strong>Focal BCE</strong> (regulariser, 30%): Pixel-level cost that prevents the Tversky component from accepting excessive false positives in exchange for recall. Adds precision pressure that improves out-of-distribution performance.</p>

<!-- ── EXPERIMENTS ────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Experimental Progression</h2>
<img class="infographic" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Optimizing Dual-Encoder UNet for EO-SAR Change Detection" width="680"/>
<p class="img-caption">Figure 3 — Four-Experiment Progression from Baseline to Best Model</p>

<div class="exp-grid">
  <div class="exp-card">
    <div class="num">Experiment 1</div>
    <div class="title">Dual-Encoder Baseline</div>
    <div class="iou">Val IoU ≈ 0.59 &nbsp;|&nbsp; Test IoU ≈ 0.27</div>
    <div class="finding">ResNet34 (EO) + ResNet18 (SAR), CBAM at bottleneck+d3/d2. Immediate 20× jump from V6 — dual-encoder hypothesis validated.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="exp-card">
    <div class="num">Experiment 2</div>
    <div class="title">Stronger Regularisation</div>
    <div class="iou">Val IoU ≈ 0.57 &nbsp;|&nbsp; Test IoU ≈ 0.26</div>
    <div class="finding">Added weight decay 3×10⁻⁴ + gradient accumulation (eff. batch=8). Essentially unchanged — training stability was not the constraint.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="exp-card key">
    <div class="num">Experiment 3 — Critical Ablation ⚠️</div>
    <div class="title">Decoder Attention Removed</div>
    <div class="iou">Val IoU ≈ 0.57 &nbsp;|&nbsp; <strong>Test IoU dropped to ≈ 0.18</strong></div>
    <div class="finding">Val stable, Test collapsed. Proves decoder CBAM is a critical generalisation mechanism that suppresses SAR speckle in unseen geographies — not just a performance booster.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="exp-card">
    <div class="num">Experiment 4 — Best Model ✓</div>
    <div class="title">Symmetric Encoders + Hybrid Loss</div>
    <div class="iou"><strong>Val IoU = 0.6050 &nbsp;|&nbsp; Test IoU = 0.3230</strong></div>
    <div class="finding">Upgraded SAR encoder to ResNet34 (symmetric capacity) + hybrid BCE+Tversky loss. Resolved EO-bias and false positive flooding simultaneously.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ── RESULTS ────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Results</h2>
<img class="infographic" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Model Performance Evolution: From Failure to Robust Generalization" width="680"/>
<p class="img-caption">Figure 4 — Model Performance Evolution: V6 Submitted Baseline → V9 Best Model</p>

<div class="results-grid">
  <div class="results-card">
    <h4>Validation Set — Scenes 01–08 (334 triplets)</h4>
    <table>
      <tr><th>Metric</th><th>Value</th></tr>
      <tr><td>IoU</td><td><strong>0.6050</strong></td></tr>
      <tr><td>F1 Score</td><td>0.7539</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Precision</td><td>0.6763</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Recall</td><td>0.8517</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Loss</td><td>0.2319</td></tr>
      <tr><td>TP / FP / FN / TN</td><td>8,751,600 / 4,189,635 / 1,523,548 / 73,091,313</td></tr>
    </table>
  </div>
  <div class="results-card">
    <h4>Test Set — Scene_09 Only, Unseen (77 triplets)</h4>
    <table>
      <tr><th>Metric</th><th>Value</th></tr>
      <tr><td>IoU</td><td><strong>0.3230</strong></td></tr>
      <tr><td>F1 Score</td><td>0.4883</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Precision</td><td>0.4197</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Recall</td><td>0.5837</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Loss</td><td>0.4828</td></tr>
      <tr><td>TP / FP / FN / TN</td><td>323,832 / 447,794 / 230,931 / 19,182,531</td></tr>
    </table>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="callout warn">
  <strong>Val-Test Gap (0.6050 → 0.3230):</strong> Primarily attributable to geographic distribution shift — scene_09 is a completely independent geography with different building density, vegetation types, and soil reflectance. Scene_09 tiles also have very low change pixel density (~2.7%) with sparse, isolated damage, structurally different from the contiguous damage zones prevalent in training data.
</div>

<!-- ── INSTALL ─────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Installation</h2>
<pre><code>git clone https://github.com/your-username/eosar-change-detection.git
cd eosar-change-detection
pip install torch torchvision tifffile albumentations</code></pre>
<p><strong>Requirements:</strong> Python 3.10+, PyTorch 2.x, CUDA-capable GPU (tested on NVIDIA T4 16GB)</p>

<!-- ── INFERENCE ──────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Inference with TTA</h2>
<pre><code>model = DualEncoderUNet(eo_channels=3, sar_channels=6).to(device)
model.load_state_dict(torch.load("best_v9.pth", map_location=device))
model.eval()

# 4-pass Test-Time Augmentation
pred_orig   = torch.sigmoid(model(images))
pred_hflip  = torch.sigmoid(model(torch.flip(images, [3]))); pred_hflip  = torch.flip(pred_hflip,  [3])
pred_vflip  = torch.sigmoid(model(torch.flip(images, [2]))); pred_vflip  = torch.flip(pred_vflip,  [2])
pred_hvflip = torch.sigmoid(model(torch.flip(images, [2,3]))); pred_hvflip = torch.flip(pred_hvflip, [2,3])

avg_pred    = (pred_orig + pred_hflip + pred_vflip + pred_hvflip) / 4.0
binary_mask = (avg_pred > 0.4).float()</code></pre>

<!-- ── LIMITATIONS ────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Known Limitations</h2>
<ul>
  <li><strong>No pre-event SAR:</strong> The cross-modal difference is a proxy for temporal change, not a direct measurement. Areas where EO and SAR naturally disagree — rough vegetation (high SAR, low EO), metal roofs (low SAR, high EO) — generate elevated false positive rates.</li>
  <li><strong>SAR speckle at structural boundaries:</strong> Double-bounce returns peak at building edges, creating sharp backscatter transitions that can exceed the classifier threshold for intact structures. A preprocessing Lee or Frost speckle filter could reduce this.</li>
  <li><strong>Single-scene test set:</strong> All 77 test tiles come from scene_09. Test metrics measure generalisation to one unseen region, not a diverse held-out distribution.</li>
</ul>

<!-- ── FUTURE WORK ────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>Future Work</h2>
<p>The primary architectural improvement identified is replacing concatenation-based fusion with <strong>bidirectional cross-encoder attention (CMAT)</strong> at each resolution scale. Rather than developing representations independently until the decoder, each encoder would query the other during encoding — the EO encoder asks "where in your SAR features does something anomalous appear that my optical features cannot explain?" and vice versa. This produces disagreement-weighted feature maps in high-dimensional learned feature space — orders of magnitude more expressive than the pixel-level cross_diff channel.</p>
<ul>
  <li>Lee/Frost speckle pre-filtering on SAR channel before feature engineering</li>
  <li>Scene-adaptive threshold calibration for unseen geographies</li>
  <li>Self-supervised pretraining on unlabelled EO-SAR pairs before supervised fine-tuning</li>
</ul>

<!-- ── REFERENCES ─────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<h2>References</h2>
<ol class="ref-list">
  <li>Ebel, P., Xu, Y., Schmitt, M., &amp; Zhu, X. X. (2021). <em>Fusing Multitemporal SAR and Optical Data for Building Damage Assessment.</em> Branch-separated dual-stream approach for heterogeneous EO-SAR comparison.</li>
  <li>Wei, J., Su, X., Wan, J., &amp; Deng, G. (2023). <em>Cross-Modal Change Detection: A Transformer-Based Mapping Network for Optical-SAR Image Pairs.</em> IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing.</li>
  <li><em>Cross-Modal Feature Interaction Network for EO-SAR Change Detection.</em> (2024). GIScience &amp; Remote Sensing. Bidirectional interaction architecture for heterogeneous sensor pairs.</li>
  <li><em>GLCD-DA: Global-Local Cross-Modal Difference Attention for SAR-Optical Change Detection.</em> (2025). ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing.</li>
  <li><em>DMoE-AttU-Net: Dual-Stream Mixture-of-Experts Attention U-Net for Change Detection.</em> (May 2025). MDPI Remote Sensing.</li>
  <li><em>M²CD: Multi-Scale Cross-Modal Change Detection with MoE Backbone and Optical-to-SAR Distillation.</em> (March 2025). IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing.</li>
</ol>

<footer>
  <p>Built for the <strong>GalaxEye Space</strong> Satellite AI Research Internship · Architecture: Dual-Encoder ResNet34-UNet with CBAM · Loss: Hybrid FocalBCE + FocalTversky · TTA: 4-pass · Threshold: 0.4</p>
</footer>

</div>
</body>
</html>
